common.offline: You are offline. Some data may be stale until the connection recovers.

auth.logoutConfirm: Are you sure you want to log out?
auth.logoutFailed: Remote logout failed. Local sign-out has been completed.

login.heroTitle: A polished admin shell for dashboards, CRUD, and extensible workflows.
login.heroDescription: Built around React best practices, theme tokens, a dynamic menu model, and a reusable workspace layout.
login.cardDescription: Use the demo account to enter the operations workspace.

layout.title: Adaptive layout
layout.description: Responsive sidebar, topbar, and tabs designed for real admin workloads.

routing.title: Dynamic routing
routing.description: Menu-driven routes and tabs with role-aware access control.

themes.title: Theme tokens
themes.description: Modern and glass visual styles with light, dark, and system modes.

layout.sidebarHint: Persisted layout preferences keep your workspace familiar across sessions.
layout.mobileHint: Tap a menu item to close the drawer.

dashboard.title: Operations dashboard
dashboard.placeholder: Monitor throughput, environment health, and adoption signals from a single command surface.
dashboard.overviewDescription: A live operations overview centered on time ranges, trend tracking, deep analysis, and plugin extensions.
dashboard.timelineDescription: Updates from shell, plugins, and automation tracks land here in a compact feed.

aiWorkbench.title: AI Workbench
aiWorkbench.placeholder: Stage prompts, test assistant configurations, and review context packages before deployment.
aiWorkbench.overviewDescription: A multi-session workspace for session management, assistant switching, context attachment, and streaming output.
aiWorkbench.promptPlaceholder: Describe the assistant behavior you want to prototype...
aiWorkbench.sendHint: Press Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a new line
aiWorkbench.contextStatusDescription: When enabled, the current page, assistant type, and session summary are attached automatically.
aiWorkbench.contextSummary: Current page: AI Workbench; assistant: {{assistant}}; session title: {{title}}.

history.userPrompt: Please review the design requirements that still needed the most work in the earliest stage of {{title}}.
history.assistantReply:
Here are the recommendations for the earliest phase:

- First narrow the core interaction goals
- Clarify empty and error states
- Reserve shortcuts for high-frequency actions

**Assigned assistant**
{{assistant}}
history.checklist:
```txt
Requirement review
Interaction walkthrough
Test coverage
```

general.name: General assistant
general.description: Handles common Q&A, writing, and solution organization.

analysis.name: Data analysis assistant
analysis.description: Focused on metrics, charts, and reporting insights.

flow.name: Flow design assistant
flow.description: Helps design node logic, branching conditions, and orchestration steps.

aiWorkbench.contextDescription: Connect language tools, retrieval adapters, and session controls without leaving the shell.

flowEditor.title: Flow editor
flowEditor.placeholder: Compose orchestration pipelines for data movement, approvals, and AI-assisted tasks.
flowEditor.listDescription: A flow list with search, filtering, pagination, and multi-action operations.

editor.restoreSuccess: Restored the most recently saved version.
editor.leaveConfirm: This flow has unsaved changes. Leave anyway?
editor.startNodeUnique: Only one start node is allowed.
editor.startNodeDuplicateBlocked: Only one start node can be kept, so duplication is blocked.
editor.startNodeSinglePreserved: Only one start node can be kept.
editor.startNodeSwitchBlocked: Only one start node is allowed, so this node cannot be changed into a start node.
editor.nodeLibraryHint: Drag onto the canvas or click the plus button
editor.canvasHint: Double-click to edit · Delete to remove · Ctrl/Cmd+Z to undo
editor.selectionEmpty: Select a node or edge to edit its properties here.

shortcuts.copyPaste: Ctrl/Cmd+C / Ctrl/Cmd+V to copy and paste nodes
shortcuts.delete: Delete removes the current node or edge

editor.nodeSheetDescription: Double-click a node or use the floating toolbar to edit it here.
editor.edgeSheetDescription: Double-click an edge label or path to open this editor.
editor.deleteDescription: The current node or edge will be removed from the canvas after deletion.

basic.title: Basic nodes
basic.description: Entry and end nodes for the flow.

flowEditor.editDescription: This layout reserves clear space for nodes, canvas tools, and property editing.

dataManagement.title: Data management
dataManagement.placeholder: Mix dashboards, forms, lists, and details inside one consistent CRUD experience.
dataManagement.nestedRouteHint: Use the nested route example to inspect list and detail pages that still share the main workspace shell.

masterDetail.title: Master-detail CRUD
masterDetail.placeholder: A practical table-plus-detail scaffold for enterprise records and nested resources.
masterDetail.listDescription: A master list that supports querying, sorting, multi-select, pagination, and batch actions.
masterDetail.advancedHint: You can extend this with amount ranges, channel types, source regions, and more filters.
masterDetail.exportSelectHint: Select at least one record before exporting.

detail.description: The main record appears in a top summary card, while sub-tables use inline, modal, and drawer editing modes.
detail.leaveConfirm: This page has unsaved changes. Leave anyway?
detail.saveFailed: Save failed. Please check the data.

errors.quantityPositiveInteger: Quantity must be a positive integer
errors.singleDefaultAddress: Exactly one default address is required
errors.trackingRequired: Tracking number is required after shipping
errors.addressRequired: Please complete the shipping address information.

detail.addressDialogDescription: Edit the shipping address in a modal dialog.
detail.logisticsDrawerDescription: Use the right-side drawer to keep the page context visible.

masterDetail.detailDescription: A hidden route still participates in tabs, breadcrumbs, and route restoration.

plugins.title: Plugin system
plugins.placeholder: Mount SystemJS-powered extensions while sharing runtime dependencies with the shell.
plugins.mountEmpty: No enabled plugins are available yet. Enable one from plugin management to render it here.
plugins.homeDescription: Connect remote extensions to the shell while keeping navigation, theme, language, and runtime APIs in sync.
plugins.homeCapabilities: This area shows how plugins can share host state, reuse shell dependencies, and render inside the same visual system.
plugins.homeSupport: Supports manifest-driven loading, shared dependency injection, route navigation, theme sync, and host notifications.
plugins.managementPageDescription: Track runtime modules, enablement, and local configuration from a single inventory view.
plugins.configureDescription: Update runtime settings stored in the local plugin manifest.
plugins.managementDescription: Track remote modules, versions, and publishing status from one inventory view.
plugins.bridgeDescription: The plugin reads host user data, theme configuration, toast APIs, current route, and navigation capabilities through @nop-chaos/plugin-bridge.
plugins.bridgeState: Shared context: theme {{theme}} / user {{user}} / language {{language}} / route {{path}} / plugin status {{status}}.

settings.title: System settings
settings.placeholder: Adjust the working environment, team defaults, and presentation rules.
settings.themeDescription: Control the design system skin and display mode without a page refresh.

classic.label: Classic
classic.description: Solid panels and clear borders for dense enterprise workflows.

glass.label: Glass
glass.description: Soft translucent surfaces with a lighter atmospheric layer.

settings.languageDescription: Persist the active locale for login and in-app navigation.
settings.layoutDescription: Choose how dense the workspace should feel on repeated visits.
settings.sidebarCollapsed: The sidebar starts in compact mode.

help.title: Help center
help.placeholder: Surface docs, onboarding steps, and release notes for end users and integrators.
help.guideDescription: Document the path from first login to plugin deployment and data review.
help.guideBody: Start with the dashboard, then move through CRUD examples, settings, and plugin management to understand how the shell behaves across routes and runtime modules.

errors.bootstrapDescription: The application could not finish startup.
errors.notFoundDescription: The route is missing or no longer exists in the active menu configuration.
errors.forbiddenDescription: Your current role set does not permit access to this route.
errors.serverDescription: The shell could not render this module. Please refresh or inspect the module registry.
